rulings.Case Title: BRUCE MAHER VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 18, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Kerr Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE ROCHA et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPOPRATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 18, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Avocet Enterprises, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium) and taken off calendar as to issue 4 (punitive damages). Mr. Rocha¿s deposition testimony and declaration, defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories and Mr. MacArthur¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Rocha was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Ventfabrics flexible duct connectors) attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE ROCHA et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPOPRATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 18, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Intervenors on behalf of their suspended insured defendant Hamilton Supply Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Mr. Rocha¿s deposition testimony and declaration, Hamilton¿s 1975 Certificate of Ownership (Intervenors¿ Exhibit O) and plaintiffs¿ Exhibits C and D create a triable issue whether Mr. Rocha was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, they create a triable issue whether Hamilton Supply Company, Inc. is liable for Oakland Sheet Metal Supply Co., Inc. during the time period at issue here. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
